title: Wendia brevivittata
taxonomy: Wendia brevivittata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This plant is native to Madagascar and belongs to the genus Wendia. Further classification places Wendia brevivittata as a member of the order Gentianales. It is also known to be a part of the subfamily Rauvolfioideae. The combination of these classifications helps in understanding the plant's position in the botanical hierarchy.

intro: Wendia brevivittata, also known as Wendia, is a plant species native to tropical regions, belonging to the family Annonaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing fever and alleviating symptoms of respiratory infections. Bioactive constituents of Wendia brevivittata include alkaloids and flavonoids, such as annonin and kaempferol, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
